title: Gantt chart is unusable due to the horizontal scroll which includes unwanted years
excerpt:
Greetings, 
Your Gantt chart is unusable due to the horizontal scroll, because it shows all the unnecessary years! 
I scroll left and I go to ex. 2004. I scroll right and I go to 2100 or even further. 
The Gantt chart sh…

posts.author: HardcoreGR (Manos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Greetings,

Your Gantt chart is unusable due to the horizontal scroll, because it shows all the unnecessary years!
I scroll left and I go to ex. 2004. I scroll right and I go to 2100 or even further.

The Gantt chart should be restricted to include ONLY the active projects’ years and nothing else!
Add a filter there! The horizontal scroll is counter-productive there in a very bad way!

Plus that sliders should be added so that we increase the viewing space of the Gantt chart without having to full-screen competely.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @HardcoreGR

Welcome to the community! Did you already find (and clicked) the “Auto Fit” in the Gantt chart?

posts.author: HardcoreGR (Manos)
posts.cookedHtml:
No can you guide me and send me a screenshot?

Thank you.

posts.author: jailynnlucidday (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @HardcoreGR !

Here is a screenshot to help guide you!

Auto Fit on Gantt1920×1017 92.6 KB

Hope this helps!

posts.author: HardcoreGR (Manos)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Autofit is irrelevant here! It just zooms the Gantt chart!
When I try to click the slider either left or right even for one millimetre, I will see years like 2010 and 2130 respectively!
What is the meaning in all this? We can’t even work.
Fix it! Add an option “Lock timeline only between active dates” or whatever, so that the Gantt chart does not go in the past and future. The scrolling is huge and the result is disappointing and counter-productive!

posts.author: codonovan (Con O'Donovan)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having the same problem and I am going to add a dummy task with a duration matching the start and end I want to work with. This will make the autofit do something helpful at least dur my initial planning phase. Then I can delete the dummy afterwards

posts.author: samcrisp (Sam Crisp)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree that the horizontal scroll is way too sensitive. Can we please reduce the sensitivity or at least create this as an option

posts.author: NoahPDX (Noah Siegel)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any resolution to this? Is there a way to freeze the view to only include the actual project timeline?

posts.author: tiggr5150 (Leigh Schroyer)
posts.cookedHtml: I am having the same issue. The Gantt chart is not usable if you have a longer chart that goes beyond the screen because the scrolling function jump too far forward or behind in years.

posts.author: Eezzee (Bryan Weston)
posts.cookedHtml: The horizontal scroll in the Gannt chart is way too sensitive and unworkable. With a mm a movement it blasts you forward a hundred years, forget about a few weeks. When using the autofit it squishes a 1 year project with approximately 30 tasks into one screen. I find the view, well squished and not too pleasant for viewing. I am really enjoying the rest of the product but the Gannt chart view really needs some overhauling.

posts.author: Tangle_Scottie (Scott Stephan)
posts.cookedHtml: Huge agree here. This is such a common and obvious UX concern! I was thinking about swapping to Monday for our Product Management, but this would make my life miserable.

posts.author: jcassano (Jay Cassano)
posts.cookedHtml: Come on guys. Fix this. It’s such a glaring issue for a product that claims to be “more” than a Project Management OS when you can’t even get Project Management right.

posts.author: Whitts (Harry Whittaker)
posts.cookedHtml: Please fix this issue

posts.author: Whitts (Harry Whittaker)
posts.cookedHtml: I will not be able to recommend this product to my project management team until this issue is resolved

posts.author: mkazmierczak (Mark Kazmierczak)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree this is terrible UI and needs to be fixed. In the meantime, when the cursor is over the chart it acts as a hand tool and you can grab and drag the Gantt chart and it’s much less sensitive.

posts.author: martinpstt (Martin Pollard)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve only recently joined Monday and I’m really tempted not to continue, because issue is so absurd. The project planning functionality is really simple and clear, but the Gantt chart is absolutely unusable because it’s impossible to scroll through our months/quarters. Please, please fix it.

posts.author: TimCoray (Coray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Engineers could look to Adobe Premiere Pro for how they address this issue in the timeline pane for video editing.

I agree with the sentiments expressed by others on this.

posts.author: ACM (Alice Macklin)
posts.cookedHtml: Another upvote for fixing this issue. It’s the biggest bug we have with the platform.

posts.author: Nlanders (Nathan Landers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why hasn’t this been fixed yet? Makes what would otherwise be a very useful tool almost unusable.

The timeline will literally jump forward to the year 2150 at the slightest touch. I don’t know about you, but I’m not planning anything 100 years+ out and I don’t even know why it would have that. Looks like it maxes out at the year 2800 or so??

I don’t understand how this would be released but it seems like an easy fix, please update pronto. Otherwise a very useful tool.

posts.author: Exquisite_Dremora (Keiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Another upvote for resolving this issue.

I was able to scroll very quickly to the year >10000, which is cool in that I can help project plan the rebuilding of House Atreides, but the real-world utility is somewhat a hindrance.

I’d suggest being able to define Start and End Dates for a project where the end date can only extend based on subordinate tasks exceeding the end date.

Bread and butter PM inclusions along w/ Task Types, Contour, Resource Schedules, and Task Dependencies (tho I hear this is coming).

posts.author: katysmith (Katy Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same issue too - the horizontal scroll is so sensitive, it’s useless, a tiny movement frequently sends you to the year 3000+ Could the width not auto scale to the length of the project plus 10% or similar?

posts.author: sam.freedlander (Sam Freedlander)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote to address this issue ASAP. It’s amazing to me that this issue has persisted for years.

posts.author: goolie (adam gould)
posts.cookedHtml: While I disagree the Gantt chart is “unusable” due to this issue, I would very much like to see it fixed, say a configuration setting allowing you to constrain Gantt chart timeline to the start/end dates of your choosing. As implemented (forever), it’s really annoying.

posts.author: sophiak (Sophia)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here. This is incredible that still hasn’t been resolved and is a key feature they sell their product on, that makes it completely unusable.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, if you are still looking for a workaround i built Gantt view app called GanttGo that lets you control the visible range.
